name: Return to the Álarcón reservoir from Honrubia
name_original: Vuelta al embalse de Álarcón desde Honrubia
region_intro: Touring cycling around Honrubia offers a diverse landscape for riders, characterized by a mix of flat plains and rounded hills. The region features significant natural landmarks such as the Gritos River Gorge and the Alarcón Reservoir, providing varied terrain and scenic views. Agricultural areas, including seasonal sunflower fields, contribute to the picturesque routes. This combination of natural and cultivated landscapes creates a range of cycling experiences. Best touring cycling routes around Honrubia The most popular touring cycling route is Return to the Álarcón reservoir from Honrubia, a 57.8 miles (93.0 km) trail that takes about 5 hours 52 minutes to complete. This route features views of the Alarcón Reservoir. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Embalse de Alarcón — circular desde Valverde de Júcar, a moderate 13.4 miles (21.6 km) path. This circular route offers additional perspectives of the Alarcón Reservoir. Local touring cyclists also love the Tébar — circular desde Cañada Juncosa, a 14.3 miles (23.0 km) trail leading through agricultural landscapes, often completed in about 1 hour 53 minutes. Touring cycling around Honrubia is defined by flat plains, rounded hills, and views of the Gritos River Gorge and Alarcón Reservoir. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes suitable for various ability levels. The routes in Honrubia are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 6 reviews. More than 30 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Honrubia's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Honrubia?
A: There are over 15 touring cycling routes available around Honrubia, catering to various skill levels. You'll find a mix of easy, moderate, and difficult options to explore the region's diverse landscapes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when touring cycling in Honrubia?
A: Honrubia offers a varied terrain, characterized by a mix of flat plains and rounded hills. You can expect routes with scenic views of natural landmarks like the Gritos River Gorge and the Alarcón Reservoir, as well as agricultural areas that include picturesque sunflower fields when in bloom.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy the most about touring cycling in Honrubia?
A: The komoot community highly rates touring cycling in Honrubia, with an average score of 5.0 stars from 6 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, from the tranquil plains to the more challenging hills, and the scenic beauty of the reservoirs and gorges.

Q: Are there easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Honrubia?
A: Yes, Honrubia offers easy touring cycling routes perfect for beginners. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1094033772/tebar-circular-desde-canada-juncosa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tébar — circular desde Cañada Juncosa&lt;/a&gt;, a 14.3-mile (23.0 km) trail that leads through agricultural landscapes and is generally completed in under two hours.

Q: Are there challenging touring cycling routes in Honrubia?
A: For those seeking a challenge, Honrubia has several difficult touring cycling routes. A notable option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1357793943/return-to-the-alarcon-reservoir-from-honrubia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Return to the Álarcón reservoir from Honrubia&lt;/a&gt;, a demanding 57.8-mile (93.0 km) trail with significant elevation changes, offering extensive views of the Alarcón Reservoir.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes around Honrubia?
A: Yes, you'll find several circular touring cycling routes. One popular option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1094045670/embalse-de-alarcon-circular-desde-valverde-de-jucar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Alarcón — circular desde Valverde de Júcar&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 13.4-mile (21.6 km) path that provides beautiful perspectives of the Alarcón Reservoir.

Q: What historical sites or natural landmarks can I explore along the cycling routes?
A: Many routes offer access to significant landmarks. You can cycle near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/963577" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alarcón Castle&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6013508" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Garcimuñoz&lt;/a&gt;. The Gritos River Gorge is a prominent natural feature, and the Roman city of Valeria, with its ancient ruins, is also nearby. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4157133" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alarcón Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; is another major natural attraction featured in several routes.

Q: Are there routes that pass through scenic natural areas like gorges?
A: Absolutely. The Gritos River Gorge is a prominent natural attraction in the region, offering scenic views and challenging segments. You can find routes that incorporate this impressive canyon, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5413205" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trail through the Gorges of Alarcón&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Honrubia?
A: The spring and autumn months generally offer the most pleasant conditions for touring cycling in Honrubia, with milder temperatures. During late spring and early summer, you might also enjoy the sight of the region's sunflower fields in full bloom.

Q: Are there family-friendly touring cycling routes in Honrubia?
A: Yes, Honrubia offers routes suitable for families, particularly those classified as easy or moderate. These routes often feature flatter sections and less traffic, allowing for a more relaxed cycling experience. Consider shorter circular routes for a family outing.

Q: Are there any routes that offer views of the Alarcón Reservoir?
A: Many routes in the area provide stunning views of the Alarcón Reservoir. Besides the longer &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1357793943/return-to-the-alarcon-reservoir-from-honrubia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Return to the Álarcón reservoir from Honrubia&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1094045670/embalse-de-alarcon-circular-desde-valverde-de-jucar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Alarcón — circular desde Valverde de Júcar&lt;/a&gt; is another excellent option to experience the reservoir's beauty.

Q: Where can I park my car when starting a touring cycling route in Honrubia?
A: Parking is generally available in and around the towns that serve as starting points for routes, such as Honrubia itself or Valverde de Júcar. For routes accessing specific natural or historical sites like the Roman city of Valeria or the Gritos River Gorge, designated parking areas are often provided nearby.

Q: Are there any other interesting places to explore near Honrubia while cycling?
A: Beyond the main routes, you can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24538256" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torrubia del Castillo – Cerro Olivilla loop from Honrubia&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through varied landscapes. The region also features the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5611845" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Henchidero Bridge&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5769666" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of the Virgen de Trascastillo&lt;/a&gt;, offering additional points of interest.
tips: The town is located on the banks of the Gritos river. On the Santo Niño promenade, you can look out from one of the enabled viewpoints and contemplate the beautiful panoramic view of the river.
On the way to the north of the Alarcón Reservoir there is this bridge that joins the Río Júcar with the swamp. Beautiful area.
Municipality located 50 km from Cuenca and 5 km from the Alarcón Reservoir. It has only 300 inhabitants. The surroundings are very beautiful and pedaling along the CM-2103 is a real pleasure.

waypoints.name: Alarcón Reservoir with the Júcar River
